svg.text: 8.16 · The transfer's lock footprint — concurrency-correct at scale
the atomic transfer (M07/7.16) seen through its locks: precise · ordered · short · non-blocking reads
the transfer's locks (held to commit, 8.10)
INSERT transaction_ (idempotency_key) → 🔒 record lock on UNIQUE index
prevents duplicate insert = the dedup mechanism (M05/5.17)
INSERT 2 ledger_entry rows → 🔒 X record + insert-intention locks (8.9)
appends to different rows → don't contend with other accounts' entries
UPDATE balance (acct A) · UPDATE balance (acct B)
→ 🔒 X RECORD lock on each PK row (tight — indexed predicate, 8.8)
atomic balance = balance ± :delta → no lost update (M07/7.11), short hold
COMMIT → all locks released together (strict 2PL, 8.10)
footprint is MINIMAL because predicates are PK/indexed (8.8)
⚠ an unindexed predicate would lock the whole table scanned
concurrency profile
⚠ DEADLOCK (8.11)
A→B vs B→A can cycle
→ fix: lock ascending account_id + retry 1213
⚠ HOT ACCOUNT (8.15)
popular acct's balance row serializes
→ short txn · shard counter · append+async
✓ MVCC reads (8.1)
reconciliation SELECT sees a snapshot
→ doesn't block / isn't blocked by transfers
level: RR (8.14)
phantom-free reads; RMW safety from
atomic update, NOT the level alone
the universal concurrency-correctness recipe
lock PRECISELY & minimally (index predicates) · in a CONSISTENT order (no cycles) · for a SHORT time (held to commit)
· with NON-BLOCKING reads (MVCC) · avoiding HOT-SPOTS (spread / append / batch)
→ same for a DB transaction, a concurrent data structure, or a distributed system. M16 scales this across shards
(keep a transfer's two accounts on ONE shard → stays a single local transaction with local locks, M02/2.16, M11)
